response: In C# you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet by using a stopwatch. This will tell you how long it took for the packet to be sent from your application. However this does not measure the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. It only measures the time from when you start sending until the send operation completes. To do this you can use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class. Create an instance of Stopwatch start it before sending the packet and stop it after the send method returns. The elapsed time will show how long the send took. This is not the same as a ping which measures round trip time. It is only one way. You can use this method to get the time it took for your application to send the packet. This approach does not involve any network round trip time. It is purely about the sending process on your side.
